steps.subgoalLabel: Ignore the decimal and multiply whole numbers
steps.explanation: First, treat the decimal number as a whole number.

steps.instruction: How many decimal places are in the original problem?
steps.work: 2.35 has 2 decimal places. 4 has 0 decimal places. Total: 2.
steps.explanation: Count the total decimal places in all factors.

steps.instruction: Put the decimal point in 940 so there are 2 decimal places.

title: Finding the Cost of 6 Items at $1.75 Each

steps.explanation: Remove the decimal and multiply as whole numbers first.

steps.instruction: 1.75 has 2 decimal places, so put the decimal 2 places from the right.
